metadata.title: The Graphic, the Publish, and the Promo
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Assignment 9 brief — Making Your Brand Unforgettable

beats.narration_text: Hola — this is Liam. Episode 5 of 6. An article nobody sees is not published — it is a draft with a URL. Part 2 of the assignment requires a custom graphic, a live published piece, and a LinkedIn promo post that earns the click. All three are separate deliverables, and all three must be in the PDF. This episode covers each one in sequence. Let's go.

props.segment: The Graphic, the Publish, and the Promo
props.command: 1 custom graphic (on-brand, not generic) + live URL (LinkedIn/Medium/Substack) + 2–3 sentence promo caption with a hook.
props.runningText: an article nobody sees isn't published — it's a draft with a URL…
output: Custom graphic: tool screenshot, branded image, or process diagram
Live URL: LinkedIn Articles / Medium / Substack
LinkedIn promo: 2–3 sentences — hook, not a summary

beats.narration_text: The graphic. The assignment requires at least one custom graphic — not a stock photo, not a placeholder image, not a default Canva template with a photo of a city skyline. A custom graphic for this article is one of four types: a tool screenshot that shows the Madison pipeline or its output in action; a branded image using your brand colors and font from Assignment 6 with a headline or pull quote from the article; a process diagram that visualizes one of the concepts in the article — the four-layer architecture, the before-and-after flow, the quality gate; or a before-and-after comparison that shows the difference the tool makes in a user's work. The tools: Canva, Adobe Express, Figma, or Midjourney for more generative options. The test: if you replaced the text on this graphic with different text, would it still look like it could be about anything? If yes, it is generic. A generic stock image costs points. The graphic should be specific enough that a reader who sees it without the article can make a reasonable guess about what the article is about. Size: at least 1200 by 628 pixels for LinkedIn. Embed it at the top of the article before you publish.

artifactLines: Type 1: tool screenshot — pipeline or output in action.
Type 2: branded image — your colors + font + headline or pull quote from the article.
Type 3: process diagram — 4-layer architecture, before/after flow, quality gate.
Type 4: before/after comparison — user's work before tool vs. after.
Generic test: if the text swapped to different content, could it be about anything? If yes — redo.
Size: 1200×628px for LinkedIn. Embed at the top before publishing.
props.sparkLine: Specific enough that a reader without the article can guess what it's about.

beats.narration_text: Three platforms to publish on: LinkedIn Articles, Medium, or Substack. All three are free. LinkedIn Articles: go to your LinkedIn profile, click 'Write an article,' paste your content, add the graphic, and publish. The article gets a URL in the format linkedin.com/pulse/your-title-your-name. This is the strongest choice if your brand is professional and the target audience is hiring managers or colleagues in the same field. Medium: create a free account at medium.com, click 'Write,' paste, publish. Medium has a built-in reader base, especially for tech and data topics. Substack: create a free newsletter at substack.com. Best choice if you want to build a list and the article is the first piece of something ongoing. After publishing, copy the URL immediately. Check that the article is publicly visible — not paywalled on Medium, not subscriber-only on Substack. Log out of your account and check the URL in an incognito window. If it is visible, the grader can read it. If it is not visible, the submission is incomplete. The URL goes in the PDF and in the Canvas submission.

artifactLines: LinkedIn: Write Article → paste → graphic → Publish. URL: linkedin.com/pulse/... Best for professional audience.
Medium: medium.com → Write → paste → Publish. Best for tech/data readers.
Substack: substack.com → free newsletter. Best if this is the first of an ongoing series.
After publish: copy URL → check in incognito window — must be publicly visible.
Paywalled or subscriber-only = incomplete submission.
props.sparkLine: Copy URL. Check in incognito. Publicly visible. Then it's published.

beats.narration_text: The LinkedIn promo caption. Two to three sentences that earn the click — not a summary, a hook. The difference: a summary tells people what the article says. A hook makes them want to find out. A summary: 'I wrote an article about building AI data pipelines and what I learned about quality gates.' A hook: 'I ran my pipeline for two weeks before I noticed the AI was prioritizing articles that mentioned the word I was tracking, even when they were entirely unrelated to the topic. Here is what I changed and why it mattered.' The hook opens a loop the reader wants to close. It names something specific that happened, or a question that the reader has not yet answered for themselves. The caption structure: one to two sentences that open the loop — the specific situation or the specific question. One sentence that tells them where to find the answer — 'the full story is in the article — link in the comments' or 'I wrote this up on LinkedIn Articles — link below.' The link itself: LinkedIn suppresses reach for posts where the link is in the caption. Put the link in the first comment instead, and add 'link in comments' at the end of the caption. That is the platform mechanic for 2026 — re-verify at build time.

artifactLines: Summary: tells people what the article says. Hook: opens a loop they want to close.
Hook structure: 1–2 sentences naming something specific that happened or a question not yet answered.
+ 1 sentence pointing to the article.
Link mechanic: put URL in the first comment; write 'link in comments' in the caption.
LinkedIn suppresses reach on posts with links in the caption. Re-verify at build time.
props.sparkLine: Hook opens a loop. Summary closes it. Write the one that earns the click.

beats.narration_text: Verdict for the Graphic, the Publish, and the Promo. One custom graphic: tool screenshot, branded image, process diagram, or before-and-after — specific enough that a reader can guess what the article is about without the text. Published live at a real URL — LinkedIn Articles, Medium, or Substack — check the URL in incognito before submitting. The LinkedIn promo caption: two to three sentences that open a loop, not a summary, with the link in the first comment. Three deliverables, all required in the PDF. Episode 6 assembles the submission.

props.artifactTitle: The Graphic, the Publish, and the Promo
artifactLines: Graphic: 1 custom (screenshot / branded / diagram / before-after). Generic stock image = costs points.
Publish: LinkedIn / Medium / Substack. Check URL in incognito — publicly visible or incomplete.
Promo caption: 2–3 sentences, hook (opens a loop) not summary. Link in first comment.
3 deliverables — all required in the PDF.
Episode 6 = final assembly + submit.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. For the promo caption, here is the prompt. Paste it into Claude after your article is published. The prompt reads: I published an article at this URL: [paste URL]. The article's turning point — the most important sentence — is: [paste it]. The article ends with this call to action: [paste CTA]. Write a LinkedIn promo caption with these requirements: two to three sentences total; the first one to two sentences open a loop using the turning point as the hook — name the specific situation or the specific question, not a summary of the article; the final sentence directs people to the article and ends with 'link in comments'; do not use phrases like 'excited to share,' 'thrilled to announce,' 'I'm passionate about,' or any filler that sounds like a template. The caption should sound like a real person describing something that actually happened. After Claude drafts it, read it out loud. If the first sentence would make you stop scrolling on LinkedIn, it is working. If it sounds like every other post you have seen in the last week, it is not done. Rewrite the first sentence in your own words before you post.

props.segment: The Graphic, the Publish, and the Promo
props.command: Article URL: [paste]. Turning point: [paste sentence]. CTA: [paste]. Write 2–3 sentence caption: hook from turning point (opens a loop) + 'link in comments.' No 'excited to share,' no summaries, no template filler.
props.runningText: test: would this first sentence make you stop scrolling? if not — rewrite it in your own words…

beats.narration_text: The Graphic, the Publish, and the Promo. Liam, in for Bear.

props.title: The Graphic, the Publish, and the Promo
